picks.reasoning: PSG won the first leg AND benefited from Chelsea's lineup being leaked beforehand — which means tonight Chelsea face them without that tactical gift. Rosenior's side now need to chase the game, opening up space for PSG on the counter. A team that just had their homework handed to them winning at a chaotic Stamford Bridge? At 3.0, this is value.

Leg 1 ended with Arsenal nicking an equaliser via a penalty so soft that Leverkusen's manager is STILL talking about it. Meanwhile their set-piece coach got chirped at for scoring from a corner — 'you do it too, eh?' — so Leverkusen arrive tonight annoyed, motivated, and apparently very good at corners. Arsenal at 1.28 to win a match that's level on aggregate against a team with a grudge? Pull the other one. Draw at 5.75 is the pick.

The press are calling City 'unbalanced and clinging to faint title hopes' — which is a kind way of saying Pep's lost the plot a bit. Real Madrid, meanwhile, have made a habit of wandering into elite European stadiums and absolutely ruining everyone's evening. They've done it to City before. At 5.75 with Guardiola's head clearly somewhere between a therapist's couch and a tactics board, Los Blancos are criminally cheap.
